explanation: The natural language states that the system should only enter Start-up Mode (StartUpMode) in response to the power button (powerButton) being pressed, after three connections have been established: breathing circuit (breathingCircuitConnected), air supply (airSupplyConnected), and power source (powerConnected), and provided the patient is not connected (¬patientConnected). This requires that: (1) StartUpMode is only initiated if all these conditions hold; (2) Whenever powerButton is pressed, if the three sources are connected and patient is not connected, then at some point (eventually) StartUpMode will become active. The most precise formalization fitting standard LTL idioms is that globally, whenever the expression 'breathingCircuitConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected & !patientConnected & powerButton' is true, then eventually StartUpMode will become true. This guarantees Start-up mode is only initiated by the power button with all connections present, and not before.

explanation_dict.natural_language: All conditions for start-up mode initiation
Start-up mode is eventually started if all conditions are true

explanation: Let's break the requirement down: 'The Start-up Mode shall be initiated by pushing the power button of the system once the system has been connected to the breathing circuit (without connection to the patient), the air supply, and the power source.' This means: Start-up Mode (StartUpMode) is only initiated (becomes true) after all of the following conditions: breathing circuit connected, air supply connected, and power connected. After those preconditions, when the power button is pressed, then StartUpMode should be initiated (i.e., StartUpMode becomes true in the next state). This is a safety property. Globally, whenever all three conditions hold, if the power button is pressed, then StartUpMode is initiated in the next state. The LTL operators used here: 'Globally' (G), 'if ... then' (->), conjunction (&), and 'next' (X).

explanation_dict.natural_language: the system has been connected to the breathing circuit
Start-up Mode is initiated in the next state
if all preconditions AND power button pressed, Start-up Mode is initiated next

output_LTL: G((breathingCircuitConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected & powerButton) -> X StartUpMode)

explanation:
We are to translate: The Start-up Mode (StartUpMode) shall be initiated by pushing the power button of the system (powerButton) ONCE both of these are true: (1) the system has been connected to the breathing circuit (breathingCircuitConnected), (2) the air supply (airSupplyConnected), and (3) the power source (powerConnected), but NOT the patient (so patientConnected is FALSE). 

In temporal logic, this requirement states: It is always the case that, if (breathingCircuitConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected & !patientConnected) holds, and then the powerButton is pressed, then eventually StartUpMode is entered. To say 'once all are connected', we want to only allow StartUpMode when those are all true, and only after the power button is pressed, so pressing powerButton is the _trigger_ for StartUpMode, but only when all connections are present.

This is often written as a response property for pressing the powerButton, restricted by a precondition:
G( (breathingCircuitConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected & !patientConnected & X powerButton) -> F StartUpMode )

However, since the mode shall ONLY be initiated under these preconditions, and only after the next press of the power button, a better translation is:
G( (breathingCircuitConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected & !patientConnected & X powerButton) -> F StartUpMode )

This means: whenever (at any time) all three are connected and patient is NOT connected, and NEXT the powerButton is pressed, then it is guaranteed eventually StartUpMode is initiated. (If there are latches/locks, the logic might need to be more strict, but this formula is the typical LTL capture for such trigger-on-precondition behavior.)

explanation_dict.natural_language: the system has been connected to the breathing circuit, the air supply, and the power source, and not yet the patient
the power button is pressed (immediately after these conditions)
if all these are true and the power button is pressed next, then the Start-up Mode will be initiated eventually
explanation_dict.LTL: (breathingCircuitConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected & !patientConnected & X powerButton) -> F StartUpMode

output_LTL: G((breathingCircuitConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected & !patientConnected & X powerButton) -> F StartUpMode)

explanation: The NL specifies the Start-up Mode (StartUpMode) shall be initiated (become true) by pushing the power button (powerButton) of the system—but only after the system has been connected to the breathing circuit (breathingCircuitConnected), the air supply (airSupplyConnected), and the power source (powerConnected), and the patient is NOT yet connected (¬patientConnected). This means: Globally, whenever the user pushes the power button, if all connections are attached (breathingCircuitConnected and airSupplyConnected and powerConnected) and patientConnected is false, then StartUpMode should be initiated (become true) in the next state (since 'initiated by' often means it holds immediately after the action). So, for every case where the connections are made, no patient is connected, and the power button is pressed, we require StartUpMode to start in the next moment. This is captured as: G(((breathingCircuitConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected & !patientConnected & powerButton) -> X StartUpMode))

explanation: The natural language sentence describes the entry into Start-up Mode being enabled iff (if and only if) all the required connections are present, and the power button is pressed (which is the trigger). The atomic propositions are: breathingCircuitConnected (system-breathing circuit connection), !patientConnected (no patient connection), airSupplyConnected, powerConnected, powerButton. The term 'once' means the power button is pressed after (possibly at the same time as) all the connections, and StartUpMode is initiated. This means: it is always required that if all the following are true: breathingCircuitConnected, airSupplyConnected, powerConnected, !patientConnected, and if the power button is pressed (powerButton), then eventually StartUpMode is (will be) active. Formally, for the duration of the system's operation, G((breathingCircuitConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected & !patientConnected & powerButton) -> F StartUpMode).

pushing the power button of the system

explanation:
We want to formalize: 'The Start-up Mode shall be initiated by pushing the power button of the system once the system has been connected to the breathing circuit (without connection to the patient), the air supply, and the power source.'

Step 1: Identify the necessary conditions before Start-up Mode may be entered—they are:
- The system must be connected to the breathing circuit ('breathingCircuitConnected')
- The system must be connected to the air supply ('airSupplyConnected')
- The power source must be connected ('powerConnected')
- The system must NOT be connected to the patient ('!patientConnected')

Step 2: The action that triggers the mode is pressing the power button ('powerButton').

Step 3: The result is Start-up Mode being initiated ('StartUpMode')

Step 4: Temporal relation: StartUpMode is initiated ONCE the above four conditions have been satisfied and when the power button is pressed. We want to say: 'whenever the power button is pressed, if the system is connected to the breathing circuit, to air supply, to power, and NOT to the patient, then StartUpMode follows.'

This is typically expressed as: whenever the conditions above and powerButton become true, then eventually StartUpMode happens (i.e., it is initiated).

So, the LTL formula is:
G((breathingCircuitConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected & !patientConnected & powerButton) -> F StartUpMode)
This states: Globally, whenever the four connection conditions and powerButton hold, eventually StartUpMode becomes true.

explanation_dict.natural_language: system is connected to breathing circuit
system is connected to air supply
system is connected to power source
system is NOT connected to patient
all preconditions and power button pressed
Once all preconditions and power button are true, eventually StartUpMode is initiated (globally)
explanation_dict.LTL: G((breathingCircuitConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected & !patientConnected & powerButton) -> F StartUpMode)

output_LTL: G((breathingCircuitConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected & !patientConnected & powerButton) -> F StartUpMode)

explanation: The natural language states that the Start-up Mode is initiated by pressing the power button, but *only after* the system has been connected to the breathing circuit, the air supply, and the power source, and before the patient is connected. This means: whenever Start-up Mode is entered, it must be after all three connections have occurred, the system's power button is then pressed, and prior to patient connection. To express this in LTL: we require (1) that StartUpMode can only occur after the connections, (2) the triggering event is a powerButton press, and (3) patientConnected is *not* true at this time. A typical formalization is: it is always the case that if StartUpMode is started, then all prerequisites must have happened before, powerButton has just been pressed, and patients are not yet connected. In LTL, assume that if StartUpMode holds now, then before it, (powerConnected & airSupplyConnected & breathingCircuitConnected & !patientConnected) were all true at the last moment, and that the triggering event is powerButton. Because LTL can't refer to the exact moment "before", we typically say globally: StartUpMode can only be entered if the three are true, patient is not connected, and powerButton has been pressed. In practice: G(StartUpMode -> (powerConnected & airSupplyConnected & breathingCircuitConnected & !patientConnected & powerButton)).

explanation_dict.natural_language: …once the system has been connected to the breathing circuit, the air supply, and the power source
If StartUpMode occurs, then all these must hold
This must hold at all times

explanation:
First, identify the main condition and actions described: The Start-up Mode ('StartUpMode') shall be initiated by pressing the power button ('powerButton'), but *only* after 1) the system has been connected to the breathing circuit ('breathingCircuitConnected'), 2) connected to air supply ('airSupplyConnected'), and 3) connected to power source ('powerConnected'). The requirement is that *when* powerButton is pressed, and once all three preconditions are true, then StartUpMode is initiated (becomes true).

This is best formalized as: Whenever powerButton is pressed, it should only be accepted if all three connections are established, and then StartUpMode is started. This can be written as: "Globally: If (powerButton is pressed and all three are connected), then StartUpMode is (immediately=true or eventually true)". Since the 'initiation' is immediate, we encode: (powerButton & breathingCircuitConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected) -> X StartUpMode.

However, to ensure StartUpMode is *only* possible after all three, we add: Not (StartUpMode) until (breathingCircuitConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected & powerButton) holds. This can alternatively be written as: StartUpMode can only become true after (powerButton & all connections), and not before.

A standard way is: G( (powerButton & breathingCircuitConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected) -> X StartUpMode ) & G( StartUpMode -> (breathingCircuitConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected))

The first conjunct ensures StartUpMode is initiated as soon as the power button is pressed and all connections are there. The second conjunct ensures that whenever StartUpMode is active, all connections are already established (i.e., cannot be in StartUpMode unless they're true).

explanation_dict.natural_language: Start-up Mode is only initiated after all connections and button press
Start-up Mode can only be active if all three connections are established

explanation_dict.LTL: G( (powerButton & breathingCircuitConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected) -> X StartUpMode ) & G( StartUpMode -> (breathingCircuitConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected))

explanation: Let's break down the sentence: 'The Start-up Mode shall be initiated by pushing the power button of the system once the system has been connected to the breathing circuit (without connection to the patient), the air supply, and the power source.' The meaning is: after all connections (breathing circuit, air supply, power source) have been made, then upon pressing the power button, the Start-up Mode will be initiated. In temporal logic: 'Once' all connections are established, 'then' upon the next press of the power button, Start-up Mode starts. Since these are system states, we use propositions: - 'breathingCircuitConnected' (system connected to the breathing circuit) - '!patientConnected' (not connected to the patient) - 'airSupplyConnected' (system connected to the air supply) - 'powerConnected' (connected to the power source) - 'powerButton' (power button is pushed) - 'StartUpMode' (Start-Up Mode is initiated)  We want to write: As soon as all the above connection conditions become true, then whenever the power button is next pressed, Start-Up Mode must be initiated. In other words, 'After all the connections are established (and patient is NOT connected), every time the power button is pressed, this must be immediately followed by initiation of StartUpMode.' In LTL, this is: G(((breathingCircuitConnected & !patientConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected) -> G(powerButton -> X StartUpMode))).  This means: globally, once the connections are made, on any subsequent powerButton press, StartUpMode must start in the next step.

system NOT connected to the patient
system connected to the air supply
system connected to the power source
if all connections established, then on any power button press, next state must be StartUpMode
once all connections are made, always: on any power button press, next state StartUpMode
explanation_dict.LTL: G((breathingCircuitConnected & !patientConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected) -> G(powerButton -> X StartUpMode))

explanation:
To translate the requirement, we break it down:

- The system must enter (initiate) Start-up Mode, but only if:
  1) The power button is pushed
  2) The breathing circuit is connected (let's deduce: connected, but without patient, so 'breathingCircuitConnected & !patientConnected')
  3) The air supply is connected ('airSupplyConnected')
  4) The power source is connected ('powerConnected')

- Since it says 'shall be initiated by pushing the power button... once the system has been connected to [all]...,' the intent is: after (or when) all these preconditions are satisfied and then the power button is pressed, Start-up Mode is entered.

- This is a typical 'if all preconditions and the event occurs (power button is pressed), then the mode is started' formula.

- In LTL, for any time, if the system is in a state where the breathing circuit is connected without the patient, air supply is connected, and power is connected, then, whenever the power button is pressed, eventually StartUpMode will become true (i.e., the mode will be initiated).

- This gives: G((breathingCircuitConnected & !patientConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected & powerButton) -> F StartUpMode)

- The global G expresses: 'always, if this situation arises, then...'

- The antecedent is all preconditions and the event of pressing power.

- The consequent is that Start-up Mode must eventually follow.

- Another way to phrase is: 'Only after all connections are made, pressing the power button will eventually start the mode.'

- This captures the requirement that the system should only start up in that condition, and whenever those are fulfilled and the button is pressed, the mode is started (possibly after some delay, as allowed by 'eventually').

all preconditions true and the power button is pressed
If all preconditions and powerButton, then eventually StartUpMode

output_LTL: G((breathingCircuitConnected & !patientConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected & powerButton) -> F StartUpMode)
